name: 13.09-create-09-manual-inputs-workflow
description: Create the 09-manual-inputs GitHub Actions workshop workflow from a natural-language specification that introduces typed manual dispatch inputs.
model: GPT-5.5 (copilot)
---

# Create 09 Manual Inputs Workflow

## Objective

Create `.github/workflows/09-manual-inputs-workflow.yml` if it has been deleted or needs to be rebuilt.

This prompt is intentionally written as a natural-language workflow request rather than a literal YAML template. Use it to demonstrate manual workflow inputs without embedding a canonical YAML answer.

## Natural-Language Workflow Specification

Create a GitHub Actions workflow named `09-manual-inputs-workflow` in `.github/workflows/09-manual-inputs-workflow.yml`.

The workflow should introduce typed `workflow_dispatch` inputs:

- Trigger on any push
- Support manual execution from the GitHub Actions UI
- Add a required manual input named `target_folder` with the description `Folder to inspect during a manual run`
- Make `target_folder` a choice input with the options `src`, `scripts`, and `.github/workflows`, and default it to `src`
- Add a required boolean manual input named `include_files` with the description `Include files in the directory listing`, defaulting to `true`
- Configure read-only repository contents permission for `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Define one job named `inspect-manual-inputs` that runs on `ubuntu-latest` with a 5-minute timeout
- Check out the repository with `actions/checkout@v4` using a step named `checkout-repository`
- Add a step named `resolve-inputs` that maps manual inputs to environment variables and falls back to `src` and `true` when the workflow was triggered by push
- Add a step named `list-requested-folder` that lists either directories and files or directories only depending on `include_files`

## Generation Requirements

1. Generate valid GitHub Actions YAML from the natural-language specification above.
2. Preserve the workflow name, trigger behavior, input names, input types, input defaults, read-only permission, job name, timeout, step names, runner, and action version.
3. Keep this workflow focused on typed manual inputs; do not add run names, event filters, artifacts, matrices, caches, concurrency, service containers, or deployment behavior.
4. Ensure push-triggered runs work even though no manual input values are supplied.

## Validation

After creating the workflow, validate `.github/workflows/09-manual-inputs-workflow.yml` with `actionlint` when available.

If validation cannot reach 0 errors, create `.github/workflows/logs/workflow-prompt-errors.md` and include:

- The failing workflow path
- The validation command used
- The full validation error output
- The likely cause
- Recommended fixes

Focused validation command:

```pwsh
$actionlint = Get-Command actionlint -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($actionlint) {
  actionlint .github/workflows/09-manual-inputs-workflow.yml
}
```
